quizzes.lessonTitle: Where to get help and stay current
quizzes.title: Asbestos Awareness for Restoration Technicians — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: What does this awareness course authorise you to do?
options: Remove non-friable asbestos up to 10 m² on any job
Sample and identify asbestos yourself
Recognise likely asbestos and respond correctly — it does NOT licence you to sample, remove or work on asbestos
Supervise a licensed asbestos removalist

questions.questionText: Can you confirm whether a material contains asbestos just by looking at it?
options: Yes, experienced technicians can always tell by sight
No — only laboratory testing can confirm it, so suspect material is presumed to be asbestos until confirmed otherwise
Yes, if it is grey and fibrous
Only if it is broken

questions.questionText: What is the difference between friable and non-friable (bonded) asbestos?
options: Friable is safe; non-friable is dangerous
They are the same thing
Friable can be crumbled to powder by hand and releases fibres very easily (higher risk); non-friable is bound in a matrix and is lower risk while intact but dangerous when broken or cut
Non-friable cannot contain asbestos

questions.questionText: Why is restoration work a particular asbestos risk?
options: Because restoration only happens in new buildings
Because water, fire and storm damage break, cut, wet and pull apart the very materials that often contain asbestos, which can release fibres
Because asbestos is added during restoration
It is not a risk at all

questions.questionText: A building from which era should be presumed to contain asbestos-containing materials unless shown otherwise?
options: Built after 2010
Built or renovated before the 2003 ban (especially before the late 1980s)
Only buildings built this year
Age makes no difference

questions.questionText: What is the safe level of asbestos exposure?
options: A small amount each day is fine
There is no known safe level of exposure — the risk comes from disturbing ACM and releasing fibres
Exposure is only harmful above a set number of hours
Any exposure outdoors is safe

questions.questionText: Under Australian WHS law, who may remove FRIABLE asbestos?
options: Any restoration technician
Anyone with a White Card
Only a removalist holding a Class A asbestos removal licence
No licence is needed for friable asbestos

questions.questionText: You should check for which document before starting work in an older building, as it records known asbestos locations?
options: The building’s original sales brochure
An asbestos register (and management plan) — workplaces built before 2004 are generally required to have one
The council rates notice
The manufacturer’s warranty

questions.questionText: You uncover broken sheeting of unknown origin in a pre-1990 bathroom during a water-damage job. What is the correct response?
options: Sand a small area to see if it is asbestos
Sweep it up quickly and bin it
Stop, do not disturb it, isolate the area, and report/escalate to a licensed asbestos assessor and, if needed, a licensed removalist — following the site’s asbestos management plan
Water-blast it to remove the dust

questions.questionText: For current, authoritative asbestos requirements on an Australian job, you should rely on:
options: US EPA asbestos guidance
Your state/territory WHS regulator, Safe Work Australia codes, and ASEA — confirming current rules for each job and location
A general internet search
Whatever the last technician told you
